case_title: NIKOLAS LEMOS VS. PROGRESSIVE CHOICE INSURANCE COMPANY et al

docket_entries.description: OPPOSITION TO DEFT'S DEMURRER TO PLTF'S COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF LEMOS, NIKOLAS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PROGRESSIVE CHOICE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION IS PROPERLY PLEADED ACCORDING TO ITS LEGAL INTENDMENT AND EFFECT. THE BAD FAITH CAUSE OF ACTION IS PROPERLY PLEADED UNDER HIGHTOWER V. FARMERS INS. EXCHANGE, 38 CAL. APP. 4TH 853 (1995). THE COMPLAINT INCLUDES SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT A PRAYER FOR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-09-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nathan L. Winegar, Esq., of Coddington, Hicks & Danforth, 650-592-5400, appearing by telephone, for Defendant and moving party
Andrew McDevitt, Esq., Walkup, Melodia, Kelly & Shoenberg, 415-981-7210, for Plaintiff and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT PROGRESSIVE CHOICE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. The breach of contract cause of action is properly pleaded according to its legal intendment and effect. The bad faith cause of action is properly pleaded under Hightower v. Farmers Ins. Exchange, 38 Cal. App. 4th 853 (1995). The complaint includes sufficient facts to support a prayer for exemplary damages. Order signed in open court (one for both motions). Judge: Marla J. Miller; Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nathan L. Winegar, Esq., of Coddington, Hicks & Danforth, 650-592-5400, appearing by telephone, for Defendant and moving party
Andrew McDevitt, Esq., Walkup, Melodia, Kelly & Shoenberg, 415-981-7210, for Plaintiff and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT PROGRESSIVE CHOICE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is denied. The breach of contract cause of action is properly pleaded according to its legal intendment and effect. The bad faith cause of action is properly pleaded under Hightower v. Farmers Ins. Exchange, 38 Cal. App. 4th 853 (1995). The complaint includes sufficient facts to support a prayer for exemplary damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
